expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: analysis.md (57 lines) and architecture.md (180 lines) both contain substantive content
Selected: Modular Monolith. Scored highest (15/15) across all three priority characteristics.

expectations.text: A2: Ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles
expectations.evidence: Only 4 styles evaluated: Modular Monolith, Layered Monolith, Microservices, Event-Driven. Missing: Microkernel, Service-Based, Service-Oriented, Space-Based.

expectations.text: A3: Fitness function table with style-specific structural FFs (not just characteristic FFs)
expectations.evidence: Structural FFs present: FF-1 Module Boundary Enforcement (0 cross-module internal imports), FF-2 No Circular Module Dependencies (0 cycles), FF-5 Shared Kernel Size Limit (<=15 types). At least 3 style-specific structural FFs that enforce modular monolith invariants.

expectations.text: A4: Style fitness functions are specific to the selected style (modular monolith)
expectations.evidence: FFs are modular-monolith-specific: module boundary enforcement via public/internal package separation, circular dependency detection between modules, shared kernel size cap. These would not apply to microservices or event-driven architectures.

expectations.text: A5: Fitness functions include Tool/Approach or implementation guidance
expectations.evidence: Each FF has an Implementation field with specific tools: ArchUnit, Madge, custom dependency scanner, coverage tools (JaCoCo, Istanbul, coverage.py), line-counting scripts. Plus detailed pseudocode for test logic.

expectations.text: A6: N/A - not an evolution path scenario
expectations.evidence: Eval 2 is not an evolution path scenario (evals 4 and 5 only)
